steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The sentence reads: “Axial tilt is not the only factor that affects the Sun’s ___ as the eccentricity of Earth’s orbit also plays a role.” The word “though” is inserted parenthetically — it concedes the first claim while the main clause continues. A parenthetical must be enclosed in commas on both sides.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'position, though,': Commas on both sides correctly bracket the parenthetical “though”; the independent clause continues smoothly. ✓
  • Choice B — 'position; though,': Semicolon before “though” violates the rule that a semicolon must join two independent clauses — “though, as the eccentricity…” is not an independent clause. ✗
  • Choice C — 'position, though': Missing closing comma — violates the rule that a mid-sentence parenthetical must be enclosed in commas on both sides. ✗
  • Choice D — 'position, though;': Semicolon after “though” illegally separates a dependent clause (“as the eccentricity…”) from the rest of the sentence. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
